nodes.label: fox | be | fox
nodes.sentence:
The fox that rode a camel

There once was a fox who lived near a garden.

nodes.label: he | slink | hole
nodes.sentence: Many a day, he would slink through a hole in the garden wall, and eat his fill of the delicious fruit that grew there.

nodes.label: he | eat | they
nodes.sentence: Juicy persimmons, tasty peaches, and delectable tangerines, he would eat them all, before trying to catch a chicken or two.

nodes.label: ∅ | go | fox
nodes.sentence: So went the days for the fox, until one morning the gardener caught sight of him, and chased him around the garden with a broomstick.

nodes.label: fox | try | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The fox tried to jump through the hole in the wall, but discovered too late that the gardener had patched the hole with fresh mud plaster, so he could not escape.fox

Seeing the gardener approaching while waving a big stick, and knowing that he had no way of escape, the fox decided to simply play dead.

nodes.label: he | drop | ground
nodes.sentence: He dropped to the ground, rolled over onto his back, and shut his eyes tight.

nodes.label: he | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: When the gardener reached the fox, he saw him just lying on the ground as if dead.

nodes.label: gardener | poke | fox
nodes.sentence: The gardener poked at the fox a few times with his stick, but the fox would not move.

nodes.label: gardener | pick | he
nodes.sentence: Assuming that the fox had died of fright, the gardener picked him up and tossed him out of the garden.

nodes.label: day | pass | ∅
nodes.sentence: Days passed, and the fox was not able to find another way back into the garden.

nodes.label: he | hatch | plan
nodes.sentence: With each day, he grew hungrier and hungier, so he hatched another plan.

nodes.label: he | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence: He decided to visit a lion he knew that lived in a nearby forest, in the hopes of eating some leftover scraps from the lion’s table.

nodes.label: he | walk | ∅
nodes.sentence: He walked and walked until he reached the lion’s den in a cave near the forest.

nodes.label: fox | sit | entrance
nodes.sentence: The fox sat near the entrance of the den and yelled out, “Hello lion!

nodes.label: it | be | fox
nodes.sentence: It is the fox here to pay you a visit, my old friend!”

nodes.label: lion | roar | ∅
nodes.sentence: “Hello fox, what brings you here and how have you been?”, the lion roared as he came out of his den to greet the fox.

nodes.label: i | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence: “I decided to walk all the way over here just to visit you.

nodes.label: fox | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: After all, what are friends for, if they can’t visit their friends once in a while,” said the fox, “but don’t ask me how I am doing, for I carry many burdens and much sadness today.”

nodes.label: fox | ask | lion
nodes.sentence: “Why, fox, what has happened?” asked the lion, only slightly concerned.

nodes.label: truth | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: “The truth is that I haven’t eaten in days, and my belly is empty because I was not able to find any food on the way over here”, replied the fox.

nodes.label: lion | say | ∅
nodes.sentence:
“Do you have anything to eat, for I have traveled a great distance to see you, and I am very hungry!”

“I am ashamed to say that I have no food for you, fox, for though I recently caught a fat boar, I’m afraid I ate him all up and have nothing left to share with you,” said the lion.

nodes.label: i | see | ∅
nodes.sentence: “However, if you can wait a bit longer, I’ll see what I can hunt in the forest tomorrow.”

nodes.label: fox | thank | lion
nodes.sentence: The fox, who was too hungry to wait another day, thanked the lion and said, “My dear lion, I don’t wish to cause you any trouble.

nodes.label: i | happen | ∅
nodes.sentence: I happen to know of a stupid and dull camel that works in a grain mill nearby.

nodes.label: we | eat | ∅
nodes.sentence: Perhaps I can trick him into coming here, so we can both eat well!”

nodes.label: lion | agree | plan
nodes.sentence: The lion agreed with the fox’s plan, and said that he would lay in wait for the camel to approach.

nodes.label: fox | have | ∅
nodes.sentence: All the fox had to do now was to go see the camel and somehow convince him to come near the lion’s den.

nodes.label: fox | go | village
nodes.sentence: So the fox went to a neighboring village, where indeed a camel worked at a grain mill.

nodes.label: camel | spend | day
nodes.sentence: The camel spent days going around and around in circles, moving the millstone which was used to grind wheat and oat into flour.

nodes.label: camel | see | ∅
nodes.sentence:
As he worked, the camel saw the fox approaching and said, “Hello fox, what brings you here?”

“Hello camel!

nodes.label: fox | say | ∅
nodes.sentence: I am happy to see you,” said the fox, cheerfully.

nodes.label: camel | thank | fox
nodes.sentence:
“I have come a long way just to visit you, old friend, for what are friends for if they don’t visit eachother once in a while?”

The camel thanked the fox for coming, and asked, “How have you been?”

“I have never been happier, camel, for I am on my way to a secret land I recently discovered, where the grass is always green, where is plenty to eat, and there’s no need to work.”

nodes.label: fox | lower | voice
nodes.sentence: Then, feigning concern, the fox lowered his voice and asked, “Tell me camel, why do you spend your days going around in circles?

nodes.label: this | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: Is this how you plan on wasting away your life away?”

nodes.label: camel | think | moment
nodes.sentence: The camel thought for moment, and replied, “I have to work to earn my keep.

nodes.label: i | turn | millstone
nodes.sentence: I turn this millstone every day from dawn ’til dusk, and in the evenings, the miller gives me some food and a safe place to sleep.

nodes.label: this | be | work
nodes.sentence: True, this is hard work, but I am content.”

nodes.label: fox | pretend | ∅
nodes.sentence: The fox pretended to go into deep thought as he absentmindedly flicked and fluffed his tail for a while, and then he excitedly said, “But I have an idea!

nodes.label: you | come | i
nodes.sentence: Why don’t you come with me to my secret land?

nodes.label: i | use | company
nodes.sentence: I can use the company, and there you will never have to grind a millstone ever again, my dearest friend!”

nodes.label: camel | stand | ∅
nodes.sentence: The camel stood still and thought this over for a while, and then agreed to accompany the fox.

nodes.label: fox | chew | yole
nodes.sentence: The fox chewed through the camel’s yole, and freed him from the millstone.

nodes.label: he | say | camel
nodes.sentence: Then he said to the camel, “My dear friend, as you know I have been walking for a long time to reach this place .

nodes.label: you | mind | ∅
nodes.sentence: Do you mind if I ride your back as I guide you to my secret land?”

nodes.label: camel | agree | this
nodes.sentence: The camel agreed to this, being an agreeable sort of camel, and the fox jumped onto the camel’s back as the two headed towards the forest.

nodes.label: fox | whisper | lie
nodes.sentence: All the while the fox whispered many lies about the secret land that he had supposedly discovered into the camel’s ear, hoping to keep the camel distracted.

nodes.label: camel | walk | ∅
nodes.sentence: The camel walked and walked until they approached the lion’s den.

nodes.label: camel | catch | sight
nodes.sentence: At that moment, the camel caught sight of the lion hiding in the bushes, and discovered the trap that was laid for him.

nodes.label: i | go | doom
nodes.sentence: “Oh, how can I go to my doom with my own four feet!”

nodes.label: camel | reproach | himself
nodes.sentence: the camel reproached himself.

nodes.label: i | find | way
nodes.sentence: “I must find a way out of this trap before it is too late!”

nodes.label: he | turn | ∅
nodes.sentence: So he immediately turned around and started walking back to the mill.

nodes.label: fox | ask | ∅
nodes.sentence:
The confused fox asked, “Why have you turned back now, camel?”

“Oh, my goodness!

nodes.label: i | forget | something
nodes.sentence: I have forgotten something very important, and I must fetch it immediately, for I cannot go anywhere without it!” cried the camel.

nodes.label: fox | yell | ∅
nodes.sentence: Frustrated, the fox yelled, “What!

nodes.label: you | forget | what
nodes.sentence: What have you forgotten which is so important?!”

nodes.label: camel | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: I must sleep with it under my pillow every night, or else I have bad dreams,” the camel replied, as he increased his pace to get away from the hiding lion as quickly as possible.

nodes.label: he | decide | ∅
nodes.sentence: The fox saw that he was powerless to stop the camel, so he decided to play along, and try to convince the camel to return to the spot the following day.

nodes.label: we | fetch | book
nodes.sentence: We will fetch your book, camel, and then we can come back tomorrow.

nodes.label: ∅ | tell | i
nodes.sentence:
But tell me, what advice did your father write down in this book which is so important?”

The camel, who was growing breathless, replied, “There were five.

nodes.label: one | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The first one was ‘Never go anywhere without this book of advice’, but I have forgotten the remaining four.

nodes.label: i | remember | they
nodes.sentence: I’ll remember them as soon as I get back to the mill.”

nodes.label: he | sigh | relief
nodes.sentence: And as soon as the camel reached the safety of the mill, he sighed in relief and said to the fox, “Ah!

nodes.label: i | remember | advice
nodes.sentence: Now I remember the other advice that my father gave me.

nodes.label: second | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The second was ‘There’s no shame in honest work.’

nodes.label: third | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The third was, ‘Be thankful for what you have.’

nodes.label: fourth | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: The fourth was ‘Never be friends with tricksters, for in the end they will trick you too.’

nodes.label: thing | be | ∅
nodes.sentence: But the fifth and most important thing my father told me was… ‘Don’t go close to lions!’

nodes.label: camel | shake | fox
nodes.sentence:
”

And having said that, the camel shook the fox off of his back, and returned to his millstone.
